description: Constitution for jagilber-dev/scripts — machine-checkable quality gates for PowerShell script development

articles.title: Code Quality
articles.description: Standards for code quality, readability, and maintainability of PowerShell scripts.

rules.text: All scripts must use approved PowerShell verbs (Get-Verb) in their filenames following Verb-Noun.ps1 convention.
All scripts must use PascalCase for script names, function names, and parameter names.
All scripts must include a .SYNOPSIS and .DESCRIPTION in comment-based help.
All scripts must pass PSScriptAnalyzer with zero errors.
Scripts should use CmdletBinding and proper parameter declarations.
Scripts may include .EXAMPLE sections demonstrating common usage patterns.
All scripts must include a Version field in .NOTES using semantic versioning (Major.Minor.Patch, e.g. 1.0.0).
All scripts must include a Changelog field in .NOTES with at least one entry per version. Newest entry at bottom.
The Version field must be incremented for any functional or instruction change: patch for fixes, minor for features, major for breaking changes.

articles.title: Security
articles.description: Security policies for credential handling, input validation, and secret management.

rules.text: Scripts must never hardcode credentials, keys, or connection strings.
Scripts must validate all external input parameters before use.
Scripts must not store secrets in plaintext files or logs.
Secrets detection must be enforced via pre-commit hooks (.secrets.baseline).
Scripts should use SecureString or Key Vault for sensitive values when applicable.

articles.title: Architecture
articles.description: Structural conventions for repository organization and script categorization.

rules.text: Scripts must be organized into category folders under powershell/ (azure, service-fabric, diagnostics, data-collection, automation, utilities).
Each script must have a corresponding Pester test file in tests/powershell/.
Azure scripts must use the Az prefix in their noun (e.g., Get-AzVmImage).
Shared utilities should be self-contained single-file scripts with no cross-script dependencies.
New categories may be added when 5 or more related scripts exist that do not fit existing categories.

articles.title: Governance
articles.description: Development workflow, review, and publication governance rules.

rules.text: All changes must be submitted via pull request to the main branch.
The dual-repo model must be followed: jagilber-dev/scripts is the canonical development source and publishes to jagilber-org/scripts (public mirror).
The public mirror must never contain .specify/, specs/, memory/, or test infrastructure.
Conventional commit messages should be used (feat:, fix:, docs:, chore:).
Constitution changes should be reviewed and constitution.md must stay in sync via sync-constitution.ps1.
Template-managed bootstrap and hook changes should be adopted as versioned deltas and recorded in .template-adoption.json.
Stable repo guidance should live under .instructions/ and be promoted through .specify/config/promotion-map.json only after validation.

articles.title: Testing
articles.description: Testing requirements and Pester test standards.

rules.text: Every script must have a Pester v5+ test file named {ScriptName}.Tests.ps1.
Tests must include Script Validation context (file exists, valid syntax, has synopsis, has description).
Tests must pass in CI (GitHub Actions) on windows-latest.
Integration tests should be tagged with 'Integration' and placed in tests/integration/ or use -Tag Integration.
Tests should follow the Arrange-Act-Assert (AAA) pattern.
External dependencies (Azure, REST APIs) should be mocked in unit tests.
